verdict: CONDITIONAL PASS - requires fixes before acceptance

critical_issues.title: Short-circuit OR logic masks field validation failures
critical_issues.description: Validation uses || (OR) short-circuit: when first field missing, second field check is skipped. If both bot_token AND chat_id are missing, only bot_token is reported as missing. Violates done_criteria 'warn 指明缺失字段' which requires all missing fields to be named.
critical_issues.example: nc.BotToken="" && nc.ChatID="" → only warns 'bot_token missing', hides 'chat_id missing'
critical_issues.impact: Users see incomplete error messages and must iterate multiple times to discover all missing fields
critical_issues.fix: Change || to && or use separate if blocks to ensure all required fields are checked and reported

critical_issues.title: Whitespace-only values bypass validation
critical_issues.description: Validation checks != "" which rejects empty strings but accepts whitespace-only: BotToken: "   " or "\t\n" passes validation. These invalid values are passed to constructors and cause silent failures at runtime when signals are sent.
critical_issues.example: BotToken: "   " → passes validation, fails at runtime
critical_issues.impact: Configuration appears valid; operator discovers problem only when signals fail to deliver
critical_issues.fix: Add strings.TrimSpace() in validation; reject trimmed-empty-string

critical_issues.title: Partial notifier failure (1 success, 1 failed) silently drops notifications
critical_issues.description: Silent-failure warn triggers only when enabled>0 AND registered==0. When enabled=2 but registered=1 (one notifier succeeds, one fails), no warning is emitted. Signals routed to the failed notifier are silently dropped.
critical_issues.example: telegram: OK, email: missing-To field → enabled=2, registered=1 → no warning → email notifications silently lost
critical_issues.impact: Operator believes all enabled notifiers are working; some notifications silently fail
critical_issues.fix: Change condition to: enabled>0 AND registered<enabled

warnings.title: Map iteration order non-determinism
warnings.description: Go maps iterate in random order. Multiple notifiers produce non-deterministic log output order
warnings.impact: Complicates troubleshooting; test assertions on log order are fragile
warnings.mitigation: Sort keys before iteration for deterministic order (optional)

warnings.title: Config key case sensitivity inconsistency
warnings.description: Config keys are case-sensitive but notifier Name() returns lowercase. Key "Telegram" doesn't match case "telegram" in switch, resulting in confusing 'unknown notifier type' error
warnings.impact: User confusion about valid key formats
warnings.mitigation: Document that keys must be lowercase, or normalize with strings.ToLower()

test_coverage_gaps: TestRegisterConfiguredNotifiers_PartialSuccess - one notifier OK, one failed, verify partial-failure warn

done_criteria_violations.criterion: Boundary[1]: 必填字段逐字段缺失表驱动...warn 指明缺失字段
done_criteria_violations.reason: Short-circuit OR logic only reports first missing field, not all

recommendations.option_a: Apply fixes (~15 LOC + 3 test functions), re-verify, then accept. Preferred to close logic gaps before production deployment.
recommendations.option_b: Accept as-is; add 3 critical + 2 warning to fast-follow backlog. Higher operational risk.

confidence: HIGH - All issues have exact line numbers and reproducible scenarios
